title: Collection compilation + operations
analysis:
This task implements collection compilation and operations across the Reify pipeline. The codebase already has: (1) Value::List/Set/Map variants in reify-types, (2) Type::List/Set/Map types, (3) syntax parsing for list/set/map literals and index access in reify-syntax, (4) stub error handlers in the compiler for collection syntax nodes, (5) apply_lambda in reify-expr for calling lambda closures.

The work involves: (A) Adding 5 new CompiledExprKind variants to reify-types (ListLiteral, SetLiteral, MapLiteral, IndexAccess, MethodCall), (B) Implementing their evaluation in reify-expr including all collection methods (count, sum, map, filter, fold, all, any, contains, concat, generate for List; count, contains, union, intersection, difference for Set; count, keys, values, contains_key for Map), (C) Implementing compilation in reify-compiler replacing the current stubs, (D) Adding dimension-aware sum to reify-stdlib.

Key constraint: The current grammar supports MemberAccess (obj.member) but not method_call with args (obj.method(args)). Property-style methods (.count, .sum, .keys, .values) compile from MemberAccess. Methods with args (.map, .filter, .fold, .contains, .union, etc.) will be tested at the CompiledExpr level directly. Full end-to-end parsing of method calls with arguments requires a grammar change (tree-sitter-reify) which is out of scope for this task's modules.

Undef propagation follows the spec: undef collection -> undef result, known-structure-unknown-elements -> count works but sum propagates, Kleene 3-valued logic for .all/.any.

prerequisites.description: Add CompiledExprKind variants (ListLiteral, SetLiteral, MapLiteral, IndexAccess, MethodCall) to reify-types/src/expr.rs. Update walk(), collect_value_refs_inner(), and add constructor helpers. ListLiteral(Vec<CompiledExpr>), SetLiteral(Vec<CompiledExpr>), MapLiteral(Vec<(CompiledExpr, CompiledExpr)>), IndexAccess { object: Box<CompiledExpr>, index: Box<CompiledExpr> }, MethodCall { object: Box<CompiledExpr>, method: String, args: Vec<CompiledExpr> }.
Update collect_body_refs_inner() in reify-compiler/src/lib.rs to handle the 5 new CompiledExprKind variants (recurse into sub-expressions for dependency tracking).

steps.description: Write failing tests in crates/reify-expr/tests/collection_eval_tests.rs for list literal evaluation: eval ListLiteral([Int(1), Int(2), Int(3)]) -> Value::List([Int(1), Int(2), Int(3)]), empty list, and list with nested expressions.

Implement eval of CompiledExprKind::ListLiteral in reify-expr/src/lib.rs -- evaluate each element expression and collect into Value::List. Add SetLiteral and MapLiteral eval simultaneously since they follow the same pattern.
Write failing tests for set literal evaluation (SetLiteral -> Value::Set with dedup) and map literal evaluation (MapLiteral -> Value::Map).
Verify set/map literal eval passes (already implemented in step-2). If not, fix.
Write failing tests for IndexAccess evaluation: list[1] -> element, list[out-of-bounds] -> Undef, map[key] -> value, map[missing-key] -> Undef, list[undef] -> Undef, undef[0] -> Undef.

Implement eval of CompiledExprKind::IndexAccess in reify-expr/src/lib.rs -- evaluate object and index, dispatch based on Value::List (Int index) and Value::Map (key lookup). Out of bounds / missing -> Undef.
Write failing tests for MethodCall .count on lists, sets, and maps: [1,2,3].count -> Int(3), set{1,2,3}.count -> Int(3), map{'a'=>1}.count -> Int(1), [].count -> Int(0).
Implement eval of CompiledExprKind::MethodCall in reify-expr/src/lib.rs -- start with .count method dispatching on Value::List/Set/Map to return their length as Value::Int.
Write failing tests for MethodCall .sum: [1,2,3].sum -> Int(6), [1.0,2.0].sum -> Real(3.0), [1mm,2mm].sum -> Scalar(0.003, LENGTH), [].sum -> Int(0), [1,undef,3].sum -> Undef.

Implement .sum method in eval_method_call -- iterate list elements, accumulate using add logic (reuse eval_add pattern from reify-expr), handle dimension-aware scalar addition. Add dimension-aware collection sum helper to reify-stdlib if needed.
Write failing tests for MethodCall .contains: [1,2,3].contains(2) -> Bool(true), [1,2,3].contains(5) -> Bool(false), set{1,2,3}.contains(2) -> Bool(true), set{1,2,3}.contains(5) -> Bool(false).
Implement .contains method for List and Set -- check if the argument value is present in the collection.
Write failing tests for MethodCall .map: [1,2,3].map(|x| x*2) -> List([2,4,6]). Construct the MethodCall with a lambda arg CompiledExpr.
Implement .map method -- evaluate the lambda arg to get Value::Lambda, then apply_lambda to each element, collect results into new Value::List.
Write failing tests for MethodCall .filter: [1,2,3,4].filter(|x| x > 2) -> List([3,4]), empty result case.
Implement .filter method -- apply_lambda to each element, keep elements where result is Bool(true), discard where Bool(false), propagate Undef.
Write failing tests for MethodCall .fold: [1,2,3].fold(0, |acc,x| acc+x) -> Int(6), fold with initial value.
Implement .fold method -- evaluate init, then for each element apply 2-arg lambda with (accumulator, element), thread result through.

Write failing tests for MethodCall .all and .any: [1,2,3].all(|x| x>0) -> Bool(true), [1,2,3].any(|x| x>2) -> Bool(true), [1,2,3].all(|x| x>2) -> Bool(false), [1,2,3].any(|x| x>5) -> Bool(false). Kleene: [1,undef,3].all(|x| x>0) -> Undef, [false,undef].all(|x| x) -> Bool(false).

Implement .all and .any methods with Kleene 3-valued logic: .all returns false if any false, undef if any undef and none false, true otherwise. .any returns true if any true, undef if any undef and none true, false otherwise.
Write failing tests for MethodCall .concat and .generate on lists: [1,2].concat([3,4]) -> List([1,2,3,4]), [].generate(3, |i| i*2) -> List([0,2,4]).
Implement .concat (merge two lists) and .generate (create list from count + lambda, applying lambda to indices 0..count) methods.
Write failing tests for set operations: set{1,2,3}.union(set{3,4}) -> Set({1,2,3,4}), set{1,2,3}.intersection(set{2,3,4}) -> Set({2,3}), set{1,2,3}.difference(set{2,3,4}) -> Set({1}).
Implement set .union, .intersection, .difference methods using BTreeSet operations.
Write failing tests for map methods: map{'a'=>1}.keys -> List(['a']), map{'a'=>1}.values -> List([1]), map{'a'=>1}.contains_key('a') -> Bool(true), map{'a'=>1}.contains_key('b') -> Bool(false).
Implement map .keys, .values (return as Value::List), .contains_key methods.

Write failing tests for undef propagation edge cases: undef.count -> Undef, [1,undef,3].count -> Int(3), [1,undef,3].sum -> Undef, undef[0] -> Undef, [1,2,3][undef] -> Undef, undef.map(lambda) -> Undef.
Verify undef propagation passes -- most cases should already work from the MethodCall dispatch checking for Undef object. Fix any gaps.

Write failing compiler tests in crates/reify-compiler/tests/collection_compile_tests.rs: compile 'let x = [1, 2, 3]' -> CompiledExprKind::ListLiteral, compile 'let x = set{1, 2}' -> SetLiteral, compile 'let x = map{"a" => 1}' -> MapLiteral, compile 'let x = items[0]' -> IndexAccess.

Implement compilation of ListLiteral, SetLiteral, MapLiteral, IndexAccess in reify-compiler -- replace the current stub error handlers with actual compilation that recursively compiles element expressions and produces the new CompiledExprKind variants.

Write failing compiler test: compile 'let n = items.count' where items is a known let binding -> CompiledExprKind::MethodCall { method: 'count', args: [] }. Test property-style method compilation from MemberAccess syntax.

Implement compilation of MemberAccess -> MethodCall in reify-compiler. When the member is a known collection method name (count, sum, keys, values) and it's NOT a port access, compile it as MethodCall with empty args. Keep port access logic intact.

Write integration test: parse + compile + eval 'structure S { let x = [1, 2, 3] }' -> verify x evaluates to Value::List([Int(1), Int(2), Int(3)]). Test parse + compile + eval for set and map literals too.
Verify integration tests pass end-to-end. Fix any issues in the parse -> compile -> eval pipeline for collection literals.
Write integration test: parse + compile + eval 'structure S { let items = [1, 2, 3]  let n = items.count }' -> verify n evaluates to Int(3). Also test list index access integration.
Verify property method and index access integration tests pass. Fix any issues.

design_decisions.decision: Add all 5 CompiledExprKind variants (ListLiteral, SetLiteral, MapLiteral, IndexAccess, MethodCall) rather than reusing existing FunctionCall for methods
design_decisions.rationale: MethodCall has a fundamentally different semantic: it dispatches on the receiver object's runtime type (List, Set, Map), not on a function name. Separate variants give clean pattern matching in the evaluator and accurate dependency tracking in collect_value_refs.

design_decisions.decision: Implement method call evaluation directly in reify-expr rather than routing through reify-stdlib
design_decisions.rationale: Collection methods need access to apply_lambda (for .map, .filter, .fold) and eval_expr (for sub-expression evaluation), which are in reify-expr. Only dimension-aware sum may delegate to stdlib for consistency with existing numeric stdlib functions.

design_decisions.decision: Test methods with arguments (.map, .filter, .fold, etc.) by constructing CompiledExpr trees directly rather than requiring end-to-end parsing
design_decisions.rationale: The current tree-sitter grammar only has MemberAccess (obj.member) with no support for method calls with arguments (obj.method(args)). A grammar change to add method_call syntax is needed but out of scope for this task's stated modules. Property-style methods (.count, .sum, .keys, .values) work through MemberAccess already.

design_decisions.decision: Compile MemberAccess to MethodCall only for known collection method names, not all member accesses
design_decisions.rationale: Port member access (port.member) must continue to compile to ValueRef as it does today. The compiler should check if the member is a known collection method name (count, sum, keys, values) and only then produce MethodCall when it's NOT a port access. Unknown members remain diagnostic errors. This preserves backward compatibility.

design_decisions.decision: Use Kleene 3-valued logic for .all and .any, consistent with existing And/Or evaluation
design_decisions.rationale: The spec requires: .all with undef element returns false if any false, undef if any undef and none false. This matches the Kleene AND/OR already implemented for BinOp::And and BinOp::Or in reify-expr.

design_decisions.decision: .sum on empty list returns Int(0) rather than Undef
design_decisions.rationale: Zero is the additive identity and the conventional sum of an empty sequence. This matches standard library behavior in most languages.

reuse.how: Reuse for .map, .filter, .fold, .all, .any, .generate -- each applies a lambda to collection elements

reuse.what: eval_add() function (addition logic with dimension awareness)
reuse.how: Reuse for .sum implementation -- iterate and accumulate using the same add semantics that handle Int+Int, Real+Real, Scalar+Scalar with dimension checking
Already implemented with PartialEq, Eq, Ord, content_hash, Display -- collection operations produce these existing Value variants
Reuse for constructing literal sub-expressions within collection compilation
Collection literal compilation follows the same pattern -- recursively compile element expressions, then wrap in the collection CompiledExprKind variant
Reuse for method call evaluation context -- same pattern as function calls, pass context through for sub-expression evaluation
Follow same pattern of constructing CompiledExpr trees and evaluating them with EvalContext::simple(). The lambda test helpers (make_value_lambda) can be adapted for collection tests.
